output:
# Weekly Leadership Report — The Westmore Hotel Dallas

**Week ending:** Sunday, May 4, 2026 (CT)
**Prepared:** Monday 8:00 AM CT for the 4:00 PM CT review

## Headline
Mid-week pace continues to improve — Sun–Tue occupancy up 7 points week-over-week thanks to two new medical contracts and steady consulting volume. ADR held at $191 USD (+$2 vs. budget). Group lead pipeline grew by **3 active opportunities** > 80 room nights.

## KPI Snapshot

| Metric | This Week | Last Week | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Occupancy | 71% | 65% | 72% | 🟡 |
| ADR | $191 | $186 | $195 | 🟡 |
| RevPAR | $135.61 | $120.90 | $140.40 | 🟡 |
| Sun–Tue Occ | 64% | 57% | 72% | 🟡 |
| Group Pipeline (RNs) | 1,420 | 1,180 | 1,500 | 🟡 |
| RFPs in flight | 7 | 5 | 8 | 🟢 |
| Outbound emails sent | 112 | 98 | 100 | 🟢 |
| Reply rate | 9.8% | 8.4% | ≥ 8% | 🟢 |

## Segment Mix (Room Nights, This Week)
- Corporate Negotiated: 41%
- Group: 18%
- Leisure / Transient: 28%
- Medical Long-Stay: 9%
- Other: 4%

## Wins
- Signed **Texas Health Resources** LNR — projected 1,200 RN/year at $165 USD
- **Methodist Health System** site visit booked for next Thursday
- 3-night group block from Dallas Bar Association confirmed (62 RNs)

## Risks
- **AT&T** RFP slipped one week — chasing for revised timeline
- Outdoor catering bookings soft for June — promotion needed
- One ADR-eroding OTA promo expires Friday, watch parity

## Asks for Leadership
1. Approve $4,500 USD for a targeted LinkedIn campaign aimed at Dallas corporate travel managers
2. Decision needed on whether to add a 5th sales call/week to UT Southwestern or hold steady
3. Sign-off on the Sun–Tue Project Team rate ladder ($165 / $179 / $189)

## Next Week's Focus
Close the AT&T RFP. Convert the Methodist site visit. Launch the win-back play to the 9–18-month dormant cohort.
